data.title: What is stagflation?
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariffs have led triggered a further discussion of stagflation in the economy, something that was already on the radar before "Liberation Day."

What is stagflation?

Stagflation is an economic phenomenon that is defined by periods with considerable inflation, little to no growth, and high unemployment. 

Periods of stagflation were prevalent in the 1970s and 1980s in most major economies, and the U.S. mainly battled with stagflation in the 1970s to early 1980s during the oil crisis.

The term was first coined by British politician Iain Macleod during a speech in 1965 to describe the U.K. economy.

Can Trump's tariff policy lead to stagflation?

Trump's tariff policies are expected by many to lead to elevated inflation and slow down economic growth. The policies could also lead to supply disruptions, which is one of the many causes of a stagflation.  

A supply shock would elevate prices for imported goods, which would then be passed on to businesses and consumers. Higher prices could lead to consumers tightening their purse strings and companies cutting costs -- slowing down economic growth and also potentially leading to layoffs/unemployment.

data.title: NextDecade surges after signing 20-year LNG supply deal with Saudi Aramco
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +13.5% pre-market Tuesday after saying it signed a 20-year liquefied natural gas sale and purchase agreement with a Saudi Aramco (ARMCO) subsidiary for offtake from Train 4 at the Rio Grande LNG facility in south Texas.

Under the deal terms, Aramco (ARMCO) will purchase 1.2M metric tons/year of LNG for 20 years on a free on board basis, at a price indexed to Henry Hub, subject to a positive final investment decision on Train 4.

Late last month, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission issued a preliminary decision supporting environmental approval for the Rio Grande LNG project.

The company recently announced expansion capacity development plans for Rio Grande LNG, including developing and beginning the permitting process for trains 6-8, with a total potential liquefaction capacity of 18M tons/year, which would lift Rio Grande LNG's total potential liquefaction capacity to 48M tons/year.

data.title: Airlines: Seeking Alpha Analysts vs. Wall Street
data.description:
Heading into the first quarter earnings season, major airlines have tempered their expectations due to softening travel demand. 

Last month, Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) warned of dipping consumer sentiment as it lowered its first-quarter guidance. A day later, American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) issued a similar distress call, projecting flat Q1 revenue growth and capacity. 

Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) and United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), too, tweaked their respective outlooks for the first quarter. 

Hashing out some optimism, SA analyst Joshua Tranchant said, "The full-year outlook is broadly unchanged for the market demand-wise, with load factors increasing YoY despite RPK (Revenue Passenger Kilometers) decreases."

In this environment, here's how Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street opinions stack up on airline stocks. 

As seen in the chart above, Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL), United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), and Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) were mostly rated Buy or higher by Wall Street and SA analysts. 

Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) featured a wide spectrum of opinions, while the sell side was cautious on JetBlue Airways (NASDAQ:JBLU), American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL), and Allegiant Travel (NASDAQ:ALGT), with SA analysts showing some divergence. 

SA and Wall Street appeared in tandem on Frontier Group (NASDAQ:ULCC) and SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW).

data.title: SANUWAVE Health sees Q1 revenue between $9.1m and $9.3M
data.description:
• SANUWAVE Health (NASDAQ:SNWV) on Wednesday said revenues for the first quarter are expected to be in the range of $9.1 to $9.3 million, an increase of 57% to 61% over Q1 2024 and slightly above the upper end of the range of its guidance.
  • SNWV +4.97% after hours to $31.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Generac unveils new line of generators targeted to data center demand
data.description:
Generac (NYSE:GNRC) +2.8% pre-market Tuesday after announcing the launch of new generators with its biggest capacity offering in North America, designed for the data center market.

The company, which currently serves smaller data center demand, said it is introducing a lineup of five new generators for the North American market, ranging from 2.25 MW to 3.25 MW - a step up from its current maximum capacity of 2 MW - that will expand its ability to support high-capacity applications while integrating into energy ecosystems.

"For decades, we've been powering the world's largest telecom operators, hospitals and many other mission critical businesses including onsite data centers," said Ricardo Navarro, senior VP and general manager of the company's global telecom and data center infrastructure section. "Coupled with our expertise in designing generators specific to a wide variety of industries and uses, this new line of generators is designed to meet the most rigorous standards for performance, packaging, and after-treatment specific to the data center market."

Generac (NYSE:GNRC) already has received commitments for the new generators for this year and the next, Navarro told Reuters in an interview.

data.title: Wells Fargo upgraded to Overweight at Piper
data.description:
Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) stock rose 4.4% in Tuesday premarket trading after Piper Sandler upgraded the stock to Overweight from Neutral as the bank moves to offense from defense and due to its quicker pace of regulatory resolution.

"Over the past few quarters, there has become a more palpable sense that WFC is no longer in 'correction' mode," analyst R. Scott Siefers wrote in a note to clients. "Rather, the company certainly seems back on offense, as evidenced by its IB gains, as well as its larger credit card ambitions and now the turn in auto from headwind to tailwind."

Also in Well's Fargo's (NYSE:WFC) favor, the Trump administration's deregulatory agenda remains intact. Furthermore, the bank has a company-specific lever in its potential asset cap removal, Siefers said. The company has had 11 consent orders closed since 2019, with five of them closed this year.

"WFC is having its most visible success in years on the regulatory front, and we expect further progress ultimately to relieve the company of what has been a significant company-specific headwind," the analyst said.

Also, with Wells Fargo's (WFC) stock dropping 13% in the past week, along with the banking sector in general, the stock trades at a more attractive entry point, he added.

Piper's Buy rating on Wells Fargo (WFC) compares with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street rating of Buy, and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating of Hold.

data.title: Morgan Stanley acts on exchanges, brokers, considering tariff-induced market volatility
data.description:
Morgan Stanley took rating actions on certain financial exchanges and brokers, considering the tariff-induced market volatility.

"All in, the current backdrop is burdened with uncertainty around economic growth, stickier inflation, path of interest rates and recession risks… which raises a greater need for hedging,trading, and risk mgmt by corporate end users and asset owners," said the investment bank in a research note.

"Further, the sharp sell-off in risk assets and period of prolonged uncertainty with rising recession risk likely weigh on investor confidence and see risk that retail investors dis-engage from themarkets," added Morgan Stanley.

Exchanges are a defensive safe haven in a period of prolonged uncertainty. Rotate towards defensive exchanges with greatest scope for upwards bias to volumes, most notably at rates, credit, and index options franchises, the research note said, upgrading CME Group (NASDAQ:CME), Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE), and MarketAxess Holdings (NASDAQ:MKTX) to Overweight.

Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) was downgraded to Equal-weight on less exposure to trading revenues that's less than 30% of firm and risks to the Solutions business growth outlook that comprise over 70% of firmwide revenues.

On brokers, the research firm said, "In the current market backdrop, we see risks that retail investors begin to disengage (for which we saw signs in March) in a period of prolonged market volatility and sharp drawdowns in broad market indices with S&P down -14% and Nasdaq down -19% YTD."

Morgan Stanley downgraded Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) to Equal-weight and Virtu Financial (NASDAQ:VIRT) to Underweight, given their exposure to the retail investor that can pull back given the sharp sell-off in risk assets, portfolio losses, potential margin calls and heightened uncertainty weighing on confidence.

At the same time, Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) was upgraded to Overweight on more defensible earnings profile, given idiosyncratic levers that can drive 20% EPS CAGR in the next two years, and greater appreciation for a high-quality franchise amidst a tough macro backdrop.

data.title: Eli Lilly looks to end selloff as Goldman upgrades on attractive entry point
data.description:
Following a three-day selloff amid market turmoil attributed to the Trump administration’s sweeping tariffs announced last week, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) shares traded higher in the premarket as Goldman Sachs upgraded the weight loss drugmaker, citing an attractive entry point.

Analyst Asad Haider, who assumed LLY’s coverage from BofA’s Chris Shibutani, upgraded the Indiana-based pharma giant to Buy from Neutral, arguing that at the current trading levels there is a “compelling entry point into the sector’s premier topline grower.”

Commenting on LLY’s leadership in the anti-obesity drug industry, where its rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) also operates, Haider said that the company is expected to maintain its pole position in a market projected to triple in size from ~$28B currently to ~$95B by 2030.

Goldman’s projections for Eli Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) weight loss drug franchise in 2026–2028 stand above the consensus at 10.3%–8.9%, thanks mainly to the company’s oral weight loss therapy orforglipron, which is currently in late-stage development.

Ahead of its Phase 3 data releases scheduled to begin in Q2 2025, Goldman projected the once-daily oral drug to generate $23.5B in risk-adjusted peak sales by 2035 in obesity and diabetes compared to $16.8B in the consensus.

The GLP-1 receptor agonist is expected to benefit from a global launch starting next year and a steep ramp, according to Hader. Additionally, the analyst noted that LLY has begun to apply the success of its obesity franchise to the remainder of its pipeline and introduce new product cycles well before the company's next major patent cliff, which is more than a decade away from now. However, Goldman Sachs revised down its price target on LLY to $888 from $892.

data.title: Inspired Entertainment attracts bull rating from B. Riley due to its multiple growth channels
data.description:
B. Riley Securities picked up coverage of Inspired Entertainment (NASDAQ:INSE) with a Buy rating. The firm thinks that as digital gaming and regulatory expansion continue to drive industry growth, Inspired Entertainment's (NASDAQ:INSE) established market position and exposure to key digital gaming trends make for a compelling investment opportunity.

Analyst Josh Nichols highlighted that content creation is one of Inspired's (INSE) core competencies. He said the company leverages an integrated-channel approach across its product portfolio, with highly synergistic land-based and digital operations. The diversified business model is seen providing stability and multiple growth avenues, with 85% of 2024 revenue coming from long-term, participation-based contracts.

"We believe the market significantly undervalues the company’s very sticky participation-based digital gaming terminal business, with a leading market position in UK and Greek retail locations and increasing penetration in North America. Meanwhile, the company’s faster-growing and high-margin digital businesses (virtual sports, interactive) were 29% of 2024 revenue, but we estimate that these will account for ~60% of total EBITDA by the end of 2025 (after corporate costs)," wrote Nichols.

Looking ahead, Nichols and his team believe INSE's digital businesses are positioned to benefit from new product and content launches, a broadening partnership base, and the growth of digital gaming in key markets. In terms of valuation, INSE was noted to trade at just 4X the 2025E EV/EBITDA multiple.

Shares of Inspired Entertainment (INSE) tracked 1.1% higher in premarket trading on Tuesday. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on INSE is flashing Strong Buy.

data.title: Nano Dimension names Ofir Baharav as CEO
data.description:
Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM) has named Ofir Baharav as its new chief executive officer, effective immediately.

This strategic move is aimed at driving merger synergies, restoring profitability, and delivering substantial shareholder value, Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM) said.

Baharav brings nearly three decades of experience in capital equipment, spanning additive manufacturing and electronics, and had already led significant changes at the firm since becoming chairman in December last year.

He has resigned from the board, while Robert Pons, a director since December, becomes the new chairman of the board.

Baharav succeeds Julien Lederman, who had been acting as interim CEO since December and will now continue as chief business officer. In addition, Zivi Nedivi, president, and Tomer Pinchas, chief financial officer and chief operating officer, are departing the company.

NNDM +4.2% premarket to $1.48.

Source: Press Release
